title: CycloneDX Blueprint Model
$comment: OWASP CycloneDX is an Ecma International standard (ECMA-424) developed in collaboration between the OWASP Foundation and Ecma Technical Committee 54 (TC54). The standard is published under a royalty-free patent policy. This JSON schema is the reference implementation and is licensed under the Apache License 2.0.

blueprints.title: Blueprints
blueprints.description: A collection of blueprints, each supporting a wide range of analytical activities, from use case documentation and behavioural analysis to comprehensive system-level threat and risk modelling.

blueprint.title: Blueprint
blueprint.description: A versatile, machine-readable model designed to support a wide range of analytical activities, from use case documentation and behavioural analysis to comprehensive system-level threat and risk modelling.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the blueprint, used to identify this model within the BOM and distinguish it from other blueprints.

description.title: Description
description.description: A detailed explanation of the blueprint, including its scope, the system or subsystem it represents, and any relevant context about the modelling objectives or intended audience.

modelTypes.title: Model Types
modelTypes.description: The types of models being represented.

assets.title: Assets
assets.description: The assets (components, services, data, actors) included in the model.

dataStores.title: Data Stores
dataStores.description: Structured description of data stores used in the model.

dataSets.title: Data Sets
dataSets.description: Datasets handled by the system, including placements and sensitivity.

zones.title: Zones
zones.description: Logical, physical, or trust zones that group assets with similar characteristics.

flows.title: Flows
flows.description: Data, control, or process flows between assets.

relationships.title: Relationships
relationships.description: Static, structural relationships between elements of the model, complementing the runtime exchanges captured by flows.

actors.title: Actors
actors.description: Human or system actors involved in the model, distinct from assets.

assumptions.title: Assumptions
assumptions.description: Assumptions made during the modeling process.

useCases.title: Use Cases

ref.title: Use Case Reference
ref.description: A reference to a use case object defined in definitions.

assets.title: Assets
assets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the blueprint assets that are involved in or exercised by this use case.

flows.title: Flows
flows.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the blueprint flows that are triggered or traversed during the execution of this use case.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the requirements that are implemented, validated, or addressed by this use case.

useCases.description: Use cases analyzed as part of the model, linking actors, goals, and steps to the blueprint's assets, flows, and requirements.

requirements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the requirements, declared in definitions, that are derived from or related to this model.

modelType.title: Model Type
modelType.description: The type of model being represented, either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom name and description for types not covered by the enumeration. Threat, risk, and privacy analyses are represented in their own models, which reference the blueprint, rather than as blueprint model types.

oneOf.title: Predefined Model Type
oneOf.description: A predefined model type from the enumeration.

meta:enum.architecture: An architecture model of the system or software, capturing its major components, their responsibilities, and the interfaces and relationships between them. Broader than a single view, it frames the overall design.
meta:enum.behavioral: A behavioural model, capturing how the system acts over time through its processes, state changes, interactions, and control flow, as opposed to its static structure.
meta:enum.conceptual: A conceptual model at the highest level of abstraction, capturing the key entities, concepts, and relationships in technology-neutral terms without implementation detail. The first level of the conceptual, logical, and physical progression.
meta:enum.data-flow: A data flow model showing how data moves between processes, data stores, and external entities. This is the substrate most commonly used for STRIDE per-element threat analysis.
meta:enum.deployment: A deployment model showing how software is allocated to runtime and infrastructure, including nodes, containers, environments, and the artifacts placed on them.
meta:enum.logical: A logical model detailing the structure and behaviour of the system independent of any specific implementation technology. The middle level of the conceptual, logical, and physical progression.
meta:enum.network: A network model showing topology and connectivity, including segments, links, and the paths over which components communicate.
meta:enum.operational: An operational model describing how the system is run and supported in production, including procedures, responsibilities, and operational dependencies.
meta:enum.physical: A physical model at the implementation level, capturing the tangible realization of the system such as hardware, devices, infrastructure, topology, and physical locations. The most concrete level of the conceptual, logical, and physical progression.
meta:enum.process: A process model describing a business or system process as an ordered set of activities and decisions and the actors that carry them out.

oneOf.title: Custom Model Type
oneOf.description: A custom model type not covered by the predefined enumeration, allowing organisations to define domain-specific model categories.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom model type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom model type.

metadata.title: Blueprint Metadata
metadata.description: Metadata about the creation, review, and approval of the blueprint.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: When the blueprint was created or last updated.

authors.title: Authors
authors.description: The parties that created the blueprint. Each entry is either an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` should include `author`.

reviewer.description: The party that reviewed the blueprint. The party's `roles` should include `reviewer`.

approver.description: The party that approved the blueprint. The party's `roles` should include `signatory` or a custom role identifying the approving authority.

validityPeriod.title: Validity Period
validityPeriod.description: The period during which the blueprint is considered valid.

reviewFrequency.title: Review Frequency
reviewFrequency.description: ISO 8601 duration for review frequency (e.g., P3M for quarterly).

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: Specifies what is included and excluded from the blueprint model, establishing the boundaries within which the modelling and analysis is performed.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the scope.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of what is included and excluded from the model.

includedComponents.title: Included Components
includedComponents.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to components explicitly included.

excludedComponents.title: Excluded Components
excludedComponents.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to components explicitly excluded.

asset.title: Asset
asset.description: A component, service, data store, actor, or other element included in the blueprint model. An actor asset references a party, since a party such as a person, persona, system, or organization may itself be an asset, including a person in the intelligence sense of a human source.

componentRef.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the component this asset represents.

serviceRef.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the service this asset represents.

partyRef.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party this asset represents, such as a person, persona, system, or organization. Used when the asset is an actor.

type.title: Asset Type
type.description: The kind of asset. Use the custom option for kinds not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.actor: An actor that is an asset in its own right, referencing a party via partyRef. A party such as a system or organization is commonly an asset, and a person may be an asset in the intelligence sense of a human source.
meta:enum.broker: Message broker or event bus infrastructure.
meta:enum.cache: Caching layer or in-memory data store.
meta:enum.data-store: Database, file system, or data repository.
meta:enum.gateway: API gateway, load balancer, or proxy.
meta:enum.tool: A tool or capability invoked by an agent, such as a function, API, or service exposed to an AI agent.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom asset type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom asset type.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the asset.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the asset's role and purpose.

zone.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the zone containing this asset.

responsibilities.title: Responsibilities
responsibilities.description: Responsibilities or functions of this asset.

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to use case objects that describe how actors interact with this asset, including the specific goals, sequences of steps, alternative and exception flows, and any requirements that govern its usage.

oneOf.title: Component Reference Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined by reference to a component declared elsewhere in the BOM.

oneOf.title: Service Reference Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined by reference to a service declared elsewhere in the BOM.

oneOf.title: Party Reference Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined by reference to a party declared elsewhere in the BOM, such as a person, persona, system, or organization that is itself an asset.

oneOf.title: Inline Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined inline within the blueprint with an explicit type and name.

dataStore.title: Data Store
dataStore.description: A persistent storage system used within the modelled system to hold, retrieve, or manage data.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the data store.

description.title: Description
description.description: Purpose and contents of the store.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of data store. Use the custom option for technologies not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.data-lake: Data lake or lakehouse for raw and semi-structured data.
meta:enum.data-warehouse: Analytical data warehouse optimised for OLAP workloads.
meta:enum.event-log: Event log or append-only streaming store.
meta:enum.hierarchical: Hierarchical or tree-structured database, such as a directory store.
meta:enum.in-memory: In-memory data store used as a primary store.
meta:enum.message-queue: Message queue or broker with persistent storage.
meta:enum.multi-model: A store supporting multiple data models.
meta:enum.search: Search index or full-text search engine.
meta:enum.vector: Vector database for embeddings and similarity search.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom data store type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom data store type.

vendor.title: Vendor
vendor.description: The vendor or publisher of the data store product.

product.title: Product
product.description: The name of the data store product.

version.title: Version
version.description: The version of the data store product.

zone.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the zone where the asset resides.

dataSets.title: Data Sets
dataSets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to data sets stored here.

dataSet.title: Data Set
dataSet.description: A logical collection of related data managed within the modelled system, including its classification, ownership, and storage placements.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the dataset.

description.title: Description
description.description: What information is contained and why it exists.

dataProfiles.title: Data Profiles
dataProfiles.description: Data profiles that govern the dataset. Each entry is either an inline profile object or a reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a previously declared profile, typically declared in the root profiles catalogue.

dataObjects.title: Data Objects
dataObjects.description: The specific data objects stored in the dataset. Each object names a payload and may reference one of the dataset's data profiles for its governing policy.

owners.title: Owners
owners.description: Parties that own the dataset. Each entry is either an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` should include `owner`.

items.title: Placement
items.description: Describes where a dataset physically or logically resides and the storage characteristics at that location.

dataStore.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the store holding this dataset.

encrypted.title: Encrypted
encrypted.description: Whether the dataset is encrypted at this location.

replicated.title: Replicated
replicated.description: Indicates if this placement is a replica.

name.description: Name of the interface.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of interface. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom interface type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom interface type.
Description of the interface.

specification.description: Link or reference to interface specification.

dataFormat.description: Data format (e.g., JSON, XML, Protocol Buffers).

zone.title: Zone
zone.description: A grouping of assets that share a common characteristic, security posture, or administrative control, so they can be reasoned about as a unit. A zone may be logical, physical, network, trust, or another kind, may nest within a parent zone, and is connected to or separated from other zones by boundaries.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the zone.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the zone's characteristics.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of zone. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.availability: An availability or fault-isolation zone, such as a cloud availability zone, used to bound the blast radius of failures and reason about redundancy and resilience.
meta:enum.compliance: A compliance scope zone whose assets fall under a specific regulatory regime, such as PCI DSS, HIPAA, or FedRAMP, delineating what is in scope and the controls applied.
meta:enum.data: A data classification zone grouping assets by the sensitivity of the data they hold or process, such as public, internal, confidential, or restricted.
meta:enum.deployment: A deployment environment grouping assets by lifecycle stage, such as development, staging, or production.
meta:enum.functional: A functional grouping of assets that serve a common purpose or capability, independent of where or how they are deployed.
meta:enum.geographic: A geographic or data-residency zone bounding assets to a region, country, or jurisdiction, used to reason about sovereignty and cross-border data flows.
meta:enum.logical: A logical grouping of assets defined by architecture or design rather than physical placement.
meta:enum.network: A network segmentation zone, such as a subnet, VLAN, or security group, within which assets share network-level reachability and controls.
meta:enum.organizational: An organizational or administrative zone grouping assets under a common owner, team, or authority.
meta:enum.physical: A physical zone bounding assets to a tangible location, such as a data centre, building, or device enclosure.
meta:enum.process: A process or execution isolation zone, such as a sandbox, container runtime, or trusted execution environment, within which code runs with a defined level of isolation.
meta:enum.tenant: A tenancy isolation zone separating the assets and data of one tenant from another in a multi-tenant system.
meta:enum.trust: A security trust zone whose assets share a common level of trust, so that movement between trust zones is where authentication, authorization, and validation are enforced.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom zone type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom zone type.

parent.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the parent zone if this is a sub-zone.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.description: Constraints or limitations of this zone.

boundary.title: Boundary
boundary.description: An edge that separates or connects two or more zones, marking a point where security controls, policies, or trust levels change. The controls expected when data or actors cross it are captured in crossingRequirements, and the threat model's trust boundary extends a boundary with trust semantics.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the boundary.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of boundary. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.data: A data classification boundary, crossed when data moves between zones of differing sensitivity, where reclassification, masking, or validation may apply.
meta:enum.functional: A functional boundary between areas of differing responsibility or capability.
meta:enum.network: A network boundary, such as a firewall, gateway, or security group, controlling reachability between network zones.
meta:enum.organizational: An organizational or administrative boundary between assets under different ownership or authority.
meta:enum.physical: A physical security boundary, such as a facility perimeter or device enclosure, between physical zones.
meta:enum.process: A process or execution boundary between isolation contexts, such as between a sandbox and its host.
meta:enum.trust: A trust boundary between zones of differing trust, the primary point at which authentication, authorization, and input validation are enforced and a key trigger for threat analysis.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom boundary type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom boundary type.

zones.title: Zones
zones.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the zones this boundary connects or separates.

crossingRequirements.title: Crossing Requirements
crossingRequirements.description: The security controls, policies, and constraints applied when data or actors cross a boundary between zones, such as the authentication and authorization enforced, the protocols permitted, and the validation, logging, and monitoring performed at the crossing.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: Authentication required to cross this boundary.

authorization.title: Authorization
authorization.description: Authorization models enforced at this boundary.

dataValidation.title: Data Validation
dataValidation.description: Whether data is validated when crossing this boundary.

dataTransformation.title: Data Transformation
dataTransformation.description: Whether data is transformed when crossing this boundary.

sessionManagement.title: Session Management
sessionManagement.description: The lifecycle of the authenticated session established at this boundary, covering session timeouts, access and refresh token validity, and logout behaviour. These properties characterize the session that results from authenticating, independently of the authentication method used.

accessTokenExpires.title: Access Token Expires
accessTokenExpires.description: Whether access tokens issued for this boundary expire.

accessTokenTtl.title: Access Token Time To Live
accessTokenTtl.description: Validity period of an access token, in seconds.

refreshToken.title: Refresh Token
refreshToken.description: Whether refresh tokens are issued for this boundary.

refreshTokenTtl.title: Refresh Token Time To Live
refreshTokenTtl.description: Validity period of a refresh token, in seconds.

idleTimeout.title: Idle Timeout
idleTimeout.description: Period of inactivity after which the session ends, in seconds.

absoluteTimeout.title: Absolute Timeout
absoluteTimeout.description: Period after session creation after which the session ends regardless of activity, in seconds.

userLogout.title: User Logout
userLogout.description: Whether a user can explicitly end the session.

systemLogout.title: System Logout
systemLogout.description: Whether the system can end the session without user action.

relationship.title: Relationship
relationship.description: A typed structural relationship from one element to one or more others, complementing the runtime exchanges captured by flows. The subject is `ref`, and the kind of relationship is expressed as a single typed property whose value lists the targets, mirroring the dependency graph for readability.

ref.title: Reference
ref.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the subject element of the relationship.

aggregates.title: Aggregates
aggregates.description: The subject is the whole in a whole-part relationship in which each target part may exist independently of the whole.

associates.title: Associates
associates.description: The subject has a general association with each target.

composes.title: Composes
composes.description: The subject is the whole in a whole-part relationship in which each target part cannot exist without the whole.

contains.title: Contains
contains.description: The subject contains or nests each target.

dependsOn.title: Depends On
dependsOn.description: The subject depends on each target.

generalizes.title: Generalizes
generalizes.description: The subject is a generalisation of each target, as in inheritance.

realizes.title: Realizes
realizes.description: The subject realises or implements each target.

serves.title: Serves
serves.description: The subject provides a service to each target.

custom.title: Custom Relationships
custom.description: Relationships whose kind is not covered by the predefined properties. Each entry names the kind and lists its targets.

type.title: Type
type.description: The name of the custom relationship kind.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: The targets of the custom relationship.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom relationship kind.

relationshipTargets.title: Relationship Targets
relationshipTargets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the target elements of a relationship.

flow.title: Flow
flow.description: Represents a data, control, or process flow between two assets, capturing the direction, type, protocols, and security controls that govern the exchange.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name or description of the flow.

description.title: Description
description.description: Detailed description of what flows and why.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of flow. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.physical: Physical movement of goods or materials.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom flow type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom flow type.

source.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the source of the flow. The source may be either an asset (such as a component, service, data store, or other element in the blueprint) or an actor that originates the exchange.

destination.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the destination of the flow. The destination may be either an asset (such as a component, service, data store, or other element in the blueprint) or an actor that receives the exchange.

bidirectional.title: Bidirectional
bidirectional.description: Whether flow occurs in both directions.

synchronous.title: Synchronous
synchronous.description: Whether the flow is synchronous or asynchronous.

encrypted.title: Encrypted
encrypted.description: Whether the data carried by this flow is encrypted in transit.

volume.title: Volume
volume.description: Expected volume or frequency of flow.

protocols.title: Protocols
protocols.description: Communication protocols used by this flow.

dataProfiles.title: Data Profiles
dataProfiles.description: Data profiles that govern the data carried by this flow. A flow may carry data governed by multiple profiles, for example a payment submission carrying both payment card data and customer personally identifiable information. Each entry is either an inline profile object or a reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a previously declared profile.

dataObjects.title: Data Objects
dataObjects.description: The specific data objects carried by this flow. Each object names a payload and may reference one of the flow's data profiles for its governing policy.

authorization.title: Authorization
authorization.description: Authorization models enforced on this flow.

sequence.title: Sequence
sequence.description: Ordering hint so flows can be assembled into sequence diagrams.

actor.title: Actor
actor.description: An actor is a contextual binding between a party and the modelled system. The party identifies who or what is acting (an organisation, person, system, or persona). The actor layer adds properties that are meaningful only within the blueprint, such as the permissions the actor holds in this system and the trust zone in which the actor operates. Identity, roles, archetype, and inter-party relations such as delegation are carried by the wrapped party and shall not be duplicated on the actor.

bom-ref.description: Unique identifier for this actor binding. References to actors from other parts of the blueprint, such as use cases or flows, use this identifier.

party.title: Party
party.description: The party that this actor binds to the modelled system. May be an inline party object declaring identity and roles in place, or a bom-link or bom-ref to a party declared elsewhere in the document.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative describing this actor's role within the modelled system, distinct from the party's identity-level description. Use to record context-specific framing such as why this actor is in scope or how it interacts with the assets in this blueprint.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: The permissions, capabilities, or duties this actor holds within the modelled system. Distinct from any innate party-level capabilities; these are scoped to the blueprint.

zone.title: Zone
zone.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the trust zone in which the actor operates.

assumption.title: Assumption
assumption.description: A stated belief or condition accepted as true for the purposes of the model, which may impact the validity of the analysis if proven false.

description.title: Description
description.description: A clear statement of the assumption being made, including the believed condition, the reasoning or evidence behind it, and any constraints or scope under which it applies.

topic.title: Topic
topic.description: The topic of the assumption. Use the custom option for topics not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.availability: Assumption about system uptime, redundancy, or failover capabilities.
meta:enum.business: Assumption about business requirements, constraints, or organizational context.
meta:enum.compliance: Assumption about regulatory, legal, or policy compliance obligations.
meta:enum.operational: Assumption about operational procedures, staffing, or maintenance practices.
meta:enum.performance: Assumption about throughput, latency, capacity, or scalability characteristics.
meta:enum.security: Assumption about threat landscape, trust boundaries, or security controls.
meta:enum.technical: Assumption about technology choices, capabilities, or technical constraints.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom assumption topic.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom assumption topic.

relatedAssets.title: Related Assets
relatedAssets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the assets that are directly affected by or dependent on this assumption, enabling impact analysis when the assumption's validity changes.

validity.title: Validity

meta:enum.invalid: The assumption has been disproven or is no longer applicable.
meta:enum.unknown: The validity of the assumption has not yet been assessed.
meta:enum.unverified: The assumption has been identified but not yet validated.
meta:enum.verified: The assumption has been confirmed as true through validation.

validity.description: The current validation state of the assumption, tracked over time to ensure that modelling decisions built on this assumption remain sound.

impact.title: Impact
impact.description: A narrative describing the consequences to the model, its assets, or the broader system if this assumption proves false, including the severity and scope of affected components.

owner.description: The party responsible for tracking, validating, and maintaining this assumption over the lifecycle of the model. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` should include `owner`.

validationMethod.title: Validation Method
validationMethod.description: The approach, test, or evidence that can be used to confirm or disprove this assumption, such as a review, audit, penetration test, or empirical measurement.

validationDate.title: Validation Date
validationDate.description: The date and time when the assumption was last validated or reviewed, used to track staleness and trigger re-evaluation.

visualization.title: Visualization
visualization.description: A visual representation of the model or a subset of its elements, capturing the diagram type, its content, and the elements depicted.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name or title of the visualization.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of what the visualization shows.

url.title: URL
url.description: URL to the diagram if stored externally.

level.title: Level

meta:enum.high: High-level view showing major components and relationships.
meta:enum.medium: Intermediate view balancing breadth and depth.
meta:enum.overview: Top-level summary providing a broad perspective of the system.

level.description: Level of detail in the visualization.

elements.title: Elements
elements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to elements shown in this visualization.

layers.title: Layers
layers.description: Layers or views available in the visualization.

visualizationType.title: Visualization Type
visualizationType.description: The type of diagram or visual representation.

oneOf.title: Predefined Visualization Type
oneOf.description: A visualization type selected from a predefined set of well-known diagram types.

meta:enum.attack-tree: Attack tree diagram showing alternative paths to a goal.
meta:enum.block: Block diagram, including SysML block definition and internal block diagrams.
meta:enum.communication: Communication diagram, formerly known as the collaboration diagram.
meta:enum.flowchart: Flowchart depicting steps or decision logic.
meta:enum.matrix: Matrix diagram, such as a dependency structure or traceability matrix.
meta:enum.mind-map: Mind map diagram for decomposition or brainstorming.
meta:enum.timing: Timing diagram showing state changes over time.

oneOf.title: Custom Visualization Type
oneOf.description: A custom visualization type not covered by the predefined enumeration.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom visualization type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom visualization type.

ownership.title: Ownership
ownership.description: Parties responsible for the asset. Each entry is either an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` carry the relationship (`owner`, `steward`, `custodian`, `end-user`, or a custom role). Multiple parties may share the same role; preference among them is expressed via `role.order`.

authenticationType.title: Authentication Type
authenticationType.description: The authentication method or protocol used to verify identity, either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom object describing a method not covered by the enumeration.

oneOf.title: Predefined Authentication Type
oneOf.description: A predefined authentication method from the enumeration.

meta:enum.basic: HTTP Basic authentication (RFC 7617), where the browser presents a native credential dialog and the username and password are sent on each request.
meta:enum.biometric: Biometric authentication, such as fingerprint or face recognition.
meta:enum.eap: Extensible Authentication Protocol, commonly used with IEEE 802.1X.
meta:enum.form: Form-based authentication, where credentials such as a username and password are submitted through an application login form rather than the browser-native HTTP Basic dialog.
meta:enum.hmac: HMAC request signing using a shared secret key.
meta:enum.magic-link: Passwordless authentication using a single-use link.
meta:enum.pin: Personal identification number, a short secret used as a knowledge factor, such as a device or application unlock PIN.
meta:enum.push: Out-of-band push approval, where a prompt is sent to a registered device or application for the user to approve or deny, optionally confirmed by matching a displayed number.
meta:enum.session-cookie: Session authentication using a server-side session identified by a cookie.

oneOf.title: Custom Authentication Type
oneOf.description: A custom authentication method not covered by the predefined enumeration, allowing organisations to describe proprietary, emerging, or composite authentication approaches.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom authentication method.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom authentication method, including how identity is verified and any relevant constraints.

authorizationType.title: Authorization Type
authorizationType.description: The authorization model used to determine and enforce access permissions, either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom object describing a model not covered by the enumeration.

oneOf.title: Predefined Authorization Type
oneOf.description: A predefined authorization model from the enumeration.

oneOf.title: Custom Authorization Type
oneOf.description: A custom authorization model not covered by the predefined enumeration, allowing organisations to describe proprietary, domain-specific, or composite access control models.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom authorization model.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom authorization model, including how access decisions are made and any relevant constraints.
